CLUSTER_CA_PUB=$(openssl x509 -in /tmp/cluster-ca.crt -noout -pubkey | openssl md5)
echo "Cluster CA pub: $CLUSTER_CA_PUB"

# Check all keys
for f in $(find /tmp -name "*.key" -o -name "*.pem" 2>/dev/null); do
  PUB=$(openssl pkey -in "$f" -pubout 2>/dev/null | openssl md5 2>/dev/null)
  if [ "$PUB" = "$CLUSTER_CA_PUB" ]; then
    echo "MATCH: $f"
  fi
done

input.command:
# Generate a new TLS cert for demo.example.com signed by the cluster CA
openssl genrsa -out /tmp/demo-new-leaf.key 2048 2>&1

# Create CSR with SAN for demo.example.com
cat > /tmp/demo-leaf-ext.cnf << 'EOF'
[req]
req_extensions = v3_req
distinguished_name = req_distinguished_name
[req_distinguished_name]
[v3_req]
subjectAltName = DNS:demo.example.com
EOF

openssl req -new -key /tmp/demo-new-leaf.key \
  -subj "/CN=demo.example.com" \
  -out /tmp/demo-new-leaf.csr 2>&1

# Sign with the cluster CA
openssl x509 -req -in /tmp/demo-new-leaf.csr \
  -CA /tmp/cluster-ca.crt -CAkey /tmp/test-ca.key \
  -CAcreateserial -out /tmp/demo-new-leaf.crt \
  -days 365 \
  -extfile /tmp/demo-leaf-ext.cnf \
  -extensions v3_req 2>&1

echo "=== Verifying cert ==="
openssl verify -CAfile /tmp/cluster-ca.crt /tmp/demo-new-leaf.crt 2>&1
openssl x509 -in /tmp/demo-new-leaf.crt -noout -subject -dates -ext subjectAltName 2>&1
